changes.before: \begin{aligned}&\left|\mathbf{S}\mathbf{o}\mathbf{l}\mathbf{u}\mathbf{l}\mathbf{i}\right.\mathbf{o}\\&\left.\begin{array}{l}\left|\mathbf{\partial}\mathbf{B}\right.\right|=\frac{\mu_0}{4\pi}\frac{I\mathbf{\partial}l\sin\theta}{r^2}[using Eq.(4.7)]\\\end{array}\right.\\&\left|\begin{array}{l}\mathbf{d}l=\Delta x=10^{-2}\mathbf{m},I=10\mathbf{A},r=0.5\mathbf{m}=y,\mu_0/4\pi=10^{-7}\frac{\mathbf{T}\mathbf{m}}{\mathbf{A}}\\\theta=90^{\circ},\therefore\sin\theta=1\\\end{array}\right.\\&\left|\begin{array}{l}\mathbf{d}\mathbf{B}\\\end{array}\right|=\frac{10^{-7}\times10\times10^{-2}}{25\times10^{-2}}=4\times10^{-8}\mathrm{T}\\&\left|\begin{array}{l}\mathbf{d}\mathbf{B}\\\end{array}\right|=\frac{10^{-7}\times10\times10^{-2}}{25\times10^{-2}}=4\times10^{-8}\mathrm{T}\\&\left|\begin{array}{l}\mathbf{d}\mathbf{I}\times\mathbf{r}=\Delta x\hat{\mathbf{i}}\times y\hat{\mathbf{j}}=y\Delta x\Big(\hat{\mathbf{i}}\times\hat{\mathbf{j}}\Big)=y\Delta x\hat{\mathbf{k}}\\\end{array}\right.\\&We remind you of the following cyclic property of cross-products,\\&\left(\hat{\mathbf{i}}\times\hat{\mathbf{j}}=\hat{\mathbf{k}};\hat{\mathbf{j}}\times\hat{\mathbf{k}}=\hat{\mathbf{i}};\hat{\mathbf{k}}\times\hat{\mathbf{k}}=\hat{\mathbf{j}}\right)\mathbf{\Delta}\mathbf{r}=\hat{\mathbf{j}}\\&Note that the field is small in magnitude.\\ \end{aligned}
changes.after: \begin{aligned}&\left|\mathbf{S}\mathbf{o}\mathbf{l}\mathbf{u}\mathbf{l}\mathbf{i}\right.\mathbf{o}\\&\left.\begin{array}{l}\left|\mathbf{\partial}\mathbf{B}\right.\right|=\frac{\mu_0}{4\pi}\frac{I\mathbf{\partial}l\sin\theta}{r^2}[using Eq.(4.7)]\\end{array}\right.\\&\left|\begin{array}{l}\mathbf{d}l=\Delta x=10^{-2}\mathbf{m},I=10\mathbf{A},r=0.5\mathbf{m}=y,\mu_0/4\pi=10^{-7}\frac{\mathbf{T}\mathbf{m}}{\mathbf{A}}\\theta=90^{\circ},\therefore\sin\theta=1\\end{array}\right.\\&\left|\begin{array}{l}\mathbf{d}\mathbf{B}\\end{array}\right|=\frac{10^{-7}\times10\times10^{-2}}{25\times10^{-2}}=4\times10^{-8}\mathrm{T}\\&\left|\begin{array}{l}\mathbf{d}\mathbf{B}\\end{array}\right|=\frac{10^{-7}\times10\times10^{-2}}{25\times10^{-2}}=4\times10^{-8}\mathrm{T}\\&\left|\begin{array}{l}\mathbf{d}\mathbf{I}\times\mathbf{r}=\Delta x\hat{\mathbf{i}}\times y\hat{\mathbf{j}}=y\Delta x\Big(\hat{\mathbf{i}}\times\hat{\mathbf{j}}\Big)=y\Delta x\hat{\mathbf{k}}\\end{array}\right.\\&We remind you of the following cyclic property of cross-products,\\&\left(\hat{\mathbf{i}}\times\hat{\mathbf{j}}=\hat{\mathbf{k}};\hat{\mathbf{j}}\times\hat{\mathbf{k}}=\hat{\mathbf{i}};\hat{\mathbf{k}}\times\hat{\mathbf{k}}=\hat{\mathbf{j}}\right)\mathbf{\Delta}\mathbf{r}=\hat{\mathbf{j}}\\&Note that the field is small in magnitude.\\ \end{aligned}
